pr.title: Match 13 misc functions (gr, it, ft, gm, if)
pr.author: johnwinston

summary: Matched a broad batch of 13 previously stubbed miscellaneous functions across stage, item, fighter, game-mode, and interface code. The PR replaced UNK stubs with typed implementations, refined several local/global structs and headers, and used a MWCC dont_inline pragma for Sound Test to preserve matching. The work matters because it converted scattered unknown callbacks and handlers into behaviorally named/typed decomp code for Rainbow Cruise, Home-Run Contest, Yoshi's Island, Tingle, Crazy Hand Bomb, Chansey, Ice Climbers ice, Popo Blizzard landing, Kirby Luigi-copy fireball, CSS data copy, regular clear result display, and Sound Test initialization/reset logic.
what_changed: Implemented ftKb_SpecialN.c function fn_800F98F4: when ftCheckThrowB0 allows firing, it gets Kirby's left-hand bone, computes world position with lb_8000B1CC, spawns It_Kind_Kirby_LuigiFire via it_802C01AC, and spawns effect 0x4B1 on the same hand joint.

Implemented ftPp_SpecialS.c function fn_80122B54 for Ice Climbers Blizzard landing: calls ftCommon_8007D7FC, transitions to ftPp_MS_SpecialLw with Fighter_ChangeMotionState, restores death/take-damage callbacks, conditionally tilts part rotation from the floor normal using atan2f, and assigns accessory4_cb to fn_80122D2C.

Implemented gm_1601.c function fn_80168A6C as a CSS-like data copy routine: zeroes a 0x20-byte output buffer, conditionally copies four s32 values from src->x0[idx], two s32 values from src->x4, and writes src->x8/src->xC into fixed output slots.

Implemented gmregclear.c function fn_8017FE54 for regular clear result display: starts image/animation-related calls, computes a bar/timer value from lbl_80472D28.x10C and x110, stores ev->x20 as a clamped 0.0F-to-1.0F fill amount, and updated gmregclear.h with a typed HSD_GObj* prototype.
Refined gmregclear.c struct lbl_80472D28_t to expose fields at offsets x2C, x10C, and x110, and added a local RegClearEv struct with HSD_ImageDesc* x1C and f32 x20.

Implemented grHomeRun_8021EA30: calculates Home-Run Contest distance using Ground_801C0498, global scale values grHr_804D6ADC/grHr_804D6AE0/grHr_804D6AE4, clamps negative distance to zero, converts for saved US language via lbLang_IsSavedLanguageUS, and stores 100x result in stage_info.x6E0.

Implemented grRCruise_80200B48: loops over 17 Rainbow Cruise platform sections, looks up each collision ID and JObj from grRc_803E4FF0, resets entry state fields, and records each platform's starting translation Y.

Implemented grYorster_80202428: if the fighter satisfies ftLib_80086960, passes a stage parameter value to ftLib_80086A4C, then finds the matching Yoshi's Island cloud/track element by item GObj and adds a supplied sag/weight value to its x4 field.

Extended gr/types.h with struct grYorster_TrackElement containing x4 and HSD_GObj* x1C, and added an elements[9] array to grYorster_GroundVars so grYorster_80202428 could index cloud platform state directly.
Implemented un_802FFCD0 in soundtest.c: copies arr[count] into arr[0..count-1], wrapped in #pragma dont_inline on/reset to stop MWCC from inlining it into callers.

Expanded itKyasarinAttributes in itCommonItems.h with xC, x30, and x34 fields, then implemented itKyasarin_UnkMotion2_Coll to switch Chansey/Kyasarin into motion 3 when it reaches left/right patrol bounds and reset x24 from attr->xC.

Implemented itClimbersice_UnkMotion2_Phys: when it_802C1854 is true, computes hitbox damage from absolute horizontal velocity scaled by special attribute x30 plus x2C, and updates the hitbox via it_80272460.

Implemented itClimbersice_UnkMotion2_Coll: runs collision helpers, checks surface contact, either stops/updates the item and subtracts life timer based on attribute x4 when speed is below/equal threshold xC, or returns true for higher-speed collision deflection behavior.

Implemented it_802F0F6C in itcrazyhandbomb.c with a typed prototype: fills SpawnItem fields, spawns a Crazy Hand bomb item, initializes it, links/debug-registers it to the owning fighter with db_80225DD8, and finalizes with it_802750F8.

Implemented it_802EB5C8 in ittincle.c: resets Tingle state flags, calls setup helpers, reads dynamic bone 11 world position, stores the vertical distance from item position as balloon/tether height, stores xC1C into item var x68, then calls it_802EB6DC. Header prototype for it_802EB6DC was also typed.
smart_moves: Kept the batch coherent by converting each stub together with its header prototype, reducing future UNK_PARAMS usage and making call sites more reliable.

Used minimal local struct definitions where full type knowledge was not yet available, such as the anonymous source struct in fn_80168A6C and RegClearEv in gmregclear.c, allowing matching without overclaiming broader layouts.
Exposed only the necessary fields in partially known structs while preserving padding and offsets, e.g. lbl_80472D28_t, itKyasarinAttributes, YorsterParams, and grYorster_TrackElement.
Used #pragma dont_inline around un_802FFCD0 because the PR notes MWCC otherwise inlined the function into callers; this is a targeted compiler-behavior fix rather than changing surrounding code.

For stage/item behavior, reused existing engine helpers instead of duplicating logic: Ground_801C32D4, Ground_801C3FA4, HSD_JObjGetTranslationY, lb_8000B1CC, Item_80268B18, db_80225DD8, it_80272460, and motion/collision helper calls.
Matched gameplay calculations directly with constants and clamps preserved, such as Home-Run Contest distance conversion and regular-clear grade bar fill clamping.
decomp_lessons: Small miscellaneous matching PRs can still require cross-system typing: item, fighter, stage, game-mode, and interface functions often need headers and structs updated together.
When a function only touches a few offsets in an unknown object, partial struct recovery with explicit padding is a safe way to improve readability while preserving layout evidence.
Header prototypes should be updated immediately after matching a function; replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with concrete signatures makes later matching and call-site inference easier.
MWCC inlining can block matches even for simple helper loops; a localized dont_inline pragma is an accepted tactic when the original binary keeps a helper out of line.

Bone/world-position helpers are recurring patterns for projectile/effect spawn functions: find a Fighter_Part or item dynamic bone, call lb_8000B1CC, then pass the resulting Vec3 to item/effect creation.
Collision and physics handlers often reveal special-attribute layout fields, especially thresholds, speed multipliers, damage values, patrol bounds, and timer deltas.
Stage ground vars often need embedded arrays sized by loop evidence, as seen with Rainbow Cruise's 17 entries and Yoshi's Island's 9 track/cloud elements.
Language-specific display math may appear in gameplay result code; preserve checks like lbLang_IsSavedLanguageUS rather than treating constants as generic scaling.
naming_conventions: Existing address-based names were retained for still-unknown functions such as fn_800F98F4, fn_80122B54, fn_80168A6C, fn_8017FE54, un_802FFCD0, and it_802F0F6C.
Stage functions continue subsystem prefixes such as grRCruise_, grHomeRun_, and grYorster_ followed by address suffixes.
Item motion callbacks use item-name plus motion/phase suffixes, e.g. itClimbersice_UnkMotion2_Phys, itClimbersice_UnkMotion2_Coll, and itKyasarin_UnkMotion2_Coll.
Struct field names remain offset-based when semantics are not fully known, such as x2C, x10C, x110, x4, x1C, x30, and x34, with explicit padding fields to preserve layout.
New local/partial structs used descriptive type names when context was clear enough: RegClearEv, YorsterParams, and grYorster_TrackElement.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Applied #pragma dont_inline on/reset to un_802FFCD0 to prevent MWCC from inlining a simple array-fill helper into callers.
Used PAD_STACK in matched functions where needed, including fn_800F98F4, fn_80122B54, and grHomeRun_8021EA30, indicating stack-shape preservation was part of the match.
Used ABS and explicit temporary variables for velocity/distance calculations in item and result code, preserving likely compiler expression ordering.
Repeated calls and reloads from gobj->user_data were preserved in fn_80122B54 and item collision code, rather than over-simplifying, to maintain codegen.
Used partial anonymous/local structs and s32 pointer casts in fn_80168A6C to match a flat 0x20-byte copy layout without prematurely defining a full global type.
Preserved float constants and branch clamps exactly, including 0.0225F, -0.175F, 0.05F, 1.0F, 70.0F, 160.0F, 100.0F, and 0.304788.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename fn_800F98F4, fn_80122B54, fn_80168A6C, fn_8017FE54, or it_802F0F6C after semantic understanding improved.
Check subsequent gr/types.h changes for a fuller definition of grYorster_GroundVars and grYorster_TrackElement beyond the fields introduced here.
Search for later gmregclear.c work refining lbl_80472D28_t and RegClearEv field names or documenting the grade bar calculation.
Find additional Sound Test matching PRs that use dont_inline pragmas or interact with un_802FFCD0 callers.
Search item attribute struct updates for itKyasarinAttributes and itClimbersIceAttributes to see whether xC/x30/x34 and speed/damage fields received semantic names.
